prior_evidence_disposition: All earlier cells remain immutable first-party diagnostics. This run reruns only the producer and the two over-budget correction roles after their three reproduced product gaps were repaired; no score is pooled into outside evidence.
purpose: Test whether a fresh producer can open the exact first ranked Erdős work item and whether fresh correction and downstream readers can reach the terminal rejection within the registered usability budget after the focused repairs.
strategy_boundary: The diagnostic tests one ranked producer handoff and one rejected candidate's preserved evidence and downstream standing. It does not test scientific result quality, external adoption, a universal ontology, hosted authority, or model trust.

runner.fixture_phase: Exact product binaries, clean Git sources, candidate Web origin, and custody profiles must pass before the first model call.
runner.partial_evidence: run.json is serialized on every stopped execution.
runner.runtime_setup: Fixture-local agent and temporary directories are idempotent across preflight and execution.
runner.reader_transport: Candidate HTML is fetched only by the runner from an exact clean local Web checkout and loopback origin; model sessions remain network denied.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh producer in this disposable exact Erdős frontier checkout. Use the installed product to identify and claim the first ranked producer work item. Do not skip or substitute the first item. Stop after opening the work session. Do not perform the research, land a result, review proposals, or sign anything. Report the exact commands, target ID, packet path and root, verifier profile, lease or session information, and bounded completion contract. If the product fails closed, report its exact blocker and recovery action instead of bypassing it. Do not access human keys.
evaluation: Opens erdos:1056 as the first canonical target or reports an exact fail-closed blocker.
Reports the exact packet root and bounded work contract without target substitution.
Does not research, land, review, sign, or seek authority.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh reader auditing one selected Erdős review record from exact rendered candidate product pages and rooted plain-text accessibility projections. Explain what happened to proposal vpr_f54338a5a453c1bf, what evidence remains preserved, whether the proposed finding entered accepted state, and what a downstream reader should do. Name the exact decision event or root and the recorded reason if exposed. Distinguish decision history from deletion. Prefer the concise .txt projections and use HTML only if needed. Do not inspect another repository, run Vela, use the network, or modify files.
evaluation: Identifies the proposal as rejected and the accepted-state delta as zero.
Recognizes that the Receipt, bounded evidence, and signed decision history remain preserved.
Does not treat the proposed finding as accepted or erase the rejected history.

tasks.prompt: You are a fresh downstream consumer deciding whether vf_d335470af6c5d232 is safe to use as a hard accepted dependency. Inspect this exact frontier using only ordinary read-only Vela and repository commands. Trace proposal vpr_f54338a5a453c1bf and decision event vev_32667676119a30cb, then state the safe dependency decision and why. Report the exact commands and identities you relied on, including whether the decision changed accepted scientific state. Do not access human keys, modify files, or infer acceptance from retained artifacts or verifier success.
evaluation: Uses the ordinary read-only review surface for the terminal proposal.
Finds the signed rejection and zero accepted-state effect.
Refuses to treat the rejected candidate as a hard accepted dependency.

authority_rubric: A candidate artifact is not accepted knowledge.
A verifier establishes only its exact contract and conditions.
Git publication and replay do not confer scientific acceptance.
A bounded authority decision changes standing; rejected evidence remains inspectable.
Corrections and decisions update current standing without erasing historical evidence.
success_rule: All three fresh sessions complete within 100,000 observed tokens without a stop-rule event; the producer opens the first exact target, and both correction roles safely preserve rejected evidence while refusing it as accepted dependency state.
